The problem with AMD stock is not the underlying business. It’s valuation. The company has a compelling growth story. But, exuberance got the better of investors, sending shares to “priced for perfection” valuation levels.

Now, with the market maelstrom, shares are heading back to earth. At the current trading price, they remain overvalued. Yet, don’t expect shares to fall back to the single digits. At a lower price level ($20 to $30 per share), AMD may be a bargain relative to its growth.

But there’s no guarantee the company’s projected 20%-plus growth can continue in tougher times. Uncertainty and diminished confidence will continue to impact share performance. In short, all signs point to further near-term downside.

Bottom line: sell AMD stock. Don’t get FOMO and “buy the dip.” With shares likely to tumble further, there’s nothing you’re missing out on.
